Technology.thesis: The AI infrastructure buildout continues to drive strong demand across semiconductors, hardware, and software, with hyperscalers and enterprises investing heavily. While some sub-industries face competitive pressures or valuation concerns, the overall sector remains a dominant force in capital allocation.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — forces a hawkish Fed repricing, killing duration bid for software
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced packaging — significant supply chain disruption

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: Consumer discretionary spending shows resilience in some areas like e-commerce and homebuilding, but broader retail faces inflation concerns and shifting consumer preferences. Auto manufacturers are navigating EV transitions and competitive pressures.
watchFor: Retail sales data shows significant contraction (e.g., -1% MoM)
Consumer confidence index drops below 90
Mortgage rates spike above 7.5% again — would cool housing demand

Communication Services.thesis: Internet content and entertainment companies are navigating AI integration and competitive landscapes. While some are finding new growth avenues, others face valuation concerns and shifting consumer engagement.
watchFor: Major tech earnings show slowdown in advertising spend
Streaming subscriber growth stalls across major platforms
New antitrust action against large internet platforms

Industrials.thesis: Aerospace & Defense continues to see strong demand due to geopolitical tensions, while heavy machinery benefits from strengthening growth drivers. Integrated freight and logistics are making strategic investments but face mixed market signals.
watchFor: De-escalation of major geopolitical conflicts
Significant cut in government defense budgets
Global trade volume contracts sharply

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive significant interest and valuation debates for leading drug manufacturers. Meanwhile, other healthcare segments like medical devices face specific challenges and downgrades.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors enter market with superior efficacy/safety
Regulatory pushback on GLP-1 pricing or access
Major healthcare policy changes (e.g., Medicare drug price negotiation expansion)

Financials.thesis: Diversified banks and credit services are showing strength, driven by strong earnings, improving loan trends, and positive analyst sentiment. Asset management firms are also seeing growth and leadership appointments.
watchFor: Unexpected Fed rate CUTS — would compress net interest margins for banks
Significant increase in loan defaults (e.g., auto, credit card)
Major regulatory changes impacting bank profitability

Consumer Defensive.thesis: Discount stores and household product companies are demonstrating resilience and attracting positive attention, driven by strong earnings and strategic initiatives.
watchFor: Significant downturn in consumer spending on essentials
Increased competition from online retailers
Rising input costs impacting margins

Utilities.thesis: Independent power producers are gaining attention as beneficiaries of the AI energy demand, with strategic investments and positive analyst outlooks driving performance.
watchFor: Slowdown in data center construction
New regulations favoring renewable energy over traditional sources
Significant increase in natural gas prices

Basic Materials.thesis: The rare earth metals industry is facing headwinds, with declining stock prices and questions about valuation despite strategic partnerships, indicating a cautious outlook.
watchFor: Significant increase in global demand for rare earth elements
New government subsidies for domestic rare earth production
Breakthroughs in rare earth mining or processing technology

Precious Metals.thesis: Gold is showing bullish momentum driven by safe-haven demand and inflation concerns, while silver faces pressure from weakening industrial demand, leading to divergent paths for the two metals.
watchFor: Sharp decline in inflation expectations
Significant strengthening of the US Dollar
Resolution of major geopolitical conflicts
